revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cs:Isuzujori-hime]][[en:Isuzuyori-hime]][[fa:ایسوزویوری-هیمه]][[ff:Isuzuyori-hime]][[ja:五十鈴依媛命]][[ko:이스즈요리히메노미코토]][[simple:Isuzuyori-hime]][[zh:五十鈴依媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|1=Emperor Suizei}}|children={{ill|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|1=Emperor Annei}}|father={{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|mother={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|qid=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Himetataraisuzuhime|qid=Q5353793|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}}; {{ill|Kamo no Kimi|qid=Q55809532|1=Kamo no Okimi}}|native_name=五十鈴依媛命}}

{{Short description|Japanese Empress}}
{{Infobox royalty
| consort      = yes
| name         = Isuzuyori-hime
| image        = 
| caption      =
| succession   = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}}
| reign        =  580–548 BC<ref name=":7">{{Cite book |last=Anston |first=W.G. |title=Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. |publisher=Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co., Limited |year=1896 |volume=1 |location=London |pages=132}}</ref>
| succession1  = [[:en:Empress dowager of Japan|Empress dowager of Japan]]
| reign1       = appointed in 548 BC<ref name=":7"/>
| spouse       = {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}
| issue        = {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}
| father       = [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]] 
| mother       = 
| religion     = [[Shinto]]
}}

'''Isuzuyori-hime''' ({{Lang-ja|五十鈴依媛}}) was the legendary empress consort of Japan as the wife of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}, the second legendary emperor of Japan. She was the mother of {{ill|Emperor Annei|en|Emperor Annei|qid=Q313345|lt=Emperor Annei|1=Emperor Annei}}. According to historical records, she is regarded as the ancestor goddess of the Masters of Shiki.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Aston |first1=W. G. |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan From the Earliest Times to A D 697 |date=18 October 2010 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-136-90234-5 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=gK4hEAAAQBAJ |language=en}}</ref>

==Life==
In the second year, during the spring season of Suizei's reign, she was appointed empress. Later on, in the first year, on the 10th month, 11th day of her son Emperor Annei's reign, he bestowed upon her the title of ''Kodaigo'' (empress dowager).<ref>{{cite book |last1=Brown |first1=Delmer |last2=Ishida |first2=Ichiro |title=The Future and the Past: A Translation and Study of the Gukansho, an Interpretative History of Japan written in 1219 |date=8 January 2021 |publisher=Univ of California Press |isbn=978-0-520-33688-9 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=gNzuDwAAQBAJ&dq=Isuzuyori-hime&pg=PA251 |language=en}}</ref> It is said that she was born as the daughter of the deity [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]],<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=「五十鈴依媛命」の用例・例文集 - 用例.jp|url=https://yourei.jp/%E4%BA%94%E5%8D%81%E9%88%B4%E4%BE%9D%E5%AA%9B%E5%91%BD|access-date=2023-01-17|website=yourei.jp}}</ref> and the sister of [[Himetataraisuzu-hime]], who was the first empress of Japan, and the first wife of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}.<ref name=":0" /><ref name="Fane">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=Annei|title=''The Imperial House of Japan''|last=[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]|publisher=Ponsonby Memorial Society|year=1959|page=29 & 418}}</ref><ref name="Brown 251">{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi|title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219|last=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida|publisher=University of California Press|year=1979|isbn=9780520034600|page=251}}</ref> She was an important mythological figure in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (Chronicles of Japan).<ref>{{cite book |title=日本書紀通釋 |date=1940 |publisher=日本書紀通釋刋行會 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=9YMfAQAAMAAJ&dq=%E4%BA%94%E5%8D%81%E9%88%B4%E4%BE%9D%E5%AA%9B%E5%91%BD&pg=PP620 |language=ja}}</ref>
